notes:
A performance & tabs release. The big theme: the app no longer freezes while it
saves. Every database write now runs on a dedicated background thread instead of
the main one, so typing in a large note — or any save, on any note — never stalls
the window. Alongside that, a broad responsiveness sweep across the editor,
journal, and sidebars, and a substantial polish + feature pass on the tab bar
(Addendum 3 §C12): overflow handling, drag-to-reorder, scroll memory, and a batch
of lifecycle fixes.

**New**

- **Drag to reorder your tabs.** Grab a tab and drop it where you want it — the
  order is yours and it sticks across launches. (Addendum 3 §C12.6. #242)
- **Tabs remember where you scrolled.** Each tab keeps its own scroll position, so
  switching away and back — or relaunching the app — drops you right back where
  you were instead of at the top. (§C12.8. #244)

**Fixes & polish**

- **The app stays responsive while saving.** Saving a large note used to briefly
  freeze the window while the database write ran on the main thread; writes now
  run off-thread, so the editor never stalls mid-keystroke. (#237)
- **Switching tabs no longer reloads the journal.** Returning to a tab used to
  re-fetch and re-render its days, scrolling you back to the top; recently-used
  tabs now stay alive in the background, so a switch is instant and keeps its
  place. (§C12.4. #243)
- **The tab bar handles a crowd gracefully.** With many tabs open, they now
  compress down to a readable minimum and then become wheel-scrollable instead of
  shrinking into unreadable slivers or pushing the "+" off-screen. (§C12.7. #240)
- **Tab lifecycle & persistence got more correct.** Closing the last tab lands on
  a fresh empty "what would you like to work on?" tab; a tab whose note was deleted
  out-of-session is cleaned up on next launch instead of lingering as a broken
  "this note no longer exists" tab; and a corrupt persisted tab can no longer fault
  navigation. (§C12.3 / §C12.8 / §C12.10. #239)
- **Tab interaction & visual polish.** Middle-click closes a tab, the right-click
  menu (close others / close to the right / duplicate) is reliable and stays
  on-screen, and the active-tab styling matches the spec. (§C12.2 / §C12.5 /
  §C12.11, Addendum 2 §B11. #241)
- **Snappier typing, scrolling, and sidebars.** A responsiveness sweep removed
  per-keystroke work that couldn't change anything, stopped off-screen journal days
  and collapsed reference cards from doing layout/paint until they're needed, and
  cut the number of live editors held open at once — so the editor, the journal,
  and both sidebars stay smooth on large workspaces. (#229–#235)

**Under the hood**

- **A dedicated writer thread for all database writes.** Every write command now
  routes through a single background actor thread that owns the write connection
  and runs writes serially (matching SQLite's single-writer model), so no write
  ever touches the main thread. On quit the actor drains its queue and commits
  before exit, with a bounded join and a closing-flag fallback — making it strictly
  safer against lost-on-quit saves than the old synchronous path, not just faster.
  Covered by a 16-case actor suite (FIFO/serial order, single-writer correctness,
  panic isolation, drain-on-shutdown, durability-after-reopen) plus a command-level
  integration test. (#237)
- **Pending editor saves flush on app quit.** A close-requested handler now awaits
  any in-flight debounced saves before the window tears down, so a keystroke typed
  microseconds before Cmd+Q still lands. (#238)
- **Heavy reads moved off the main thread, too.** A codebase-wide audit routed the
  remaining backlinks/todos/blocks and other heavy read commands onto fresh
  WAL-concurrent read connections off the main thread, and de-N+1'd the backlinks
  query. (#234, #236)
- **Off-screen content opts out of layout/paint.** Journal days and reference
  sidebar cards that are scrolled out of view use `content-visibility` so the
  engine can skip their work; the floating toolbar is portaled so it isn't trapped
  by that containment. (#231, #233)
- **The mounted tab-body set is LRU-capped.** Keeping every visited tab alive grew
  memory unbounded (each holds live editors); the active tab plus the most-recently
  used few stay mounted, and the rest unmount and re-hydrate on return. (§C12.4.
  #243)
- **Smaller wins.** Image resize commits once instead of on every drag frame, the
  All-Notes list windows its rows, a hidden Todos view stops refetching, and the
  sidebar's structural refetches and global toolbar listeners are scoped to the
  focused editor. (#232, #235)
